fresh_facts.claim: An Iranian oil tanker named Sili City breached the US naval blockade in the Arabian Sea under IRGC protection and entered Iranian waters on April 22, 2026.
President Trump extended a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran conditionally while warning of airstrikes if diplomacy fails, stating the US military is prepared for action.
Iran rejected joining diplomatic talks in Pakistan, with negotiations stalled amid US threats of airstrikes.
A US naval vessel seized an Iranian cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, heightening tensions in the region.
Iran's judiciary chief Gholam Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i called for complete readiness of all forces against the US-Israeli war coalition.
Iran's UN ambassador stated on April 22, 2026 that Tehran will hold the next round of talks in Islamabad as soon as the US lifts its blockade.
A fragile truce between Tehran and Washington was set to expire on April 22, 2026, potentially ending restraint and resuming conflict.
President Trump stated on April 21, 2026 that Iran is collapsing financially and losing $500 million daily.
Iran reversed a Hormuz reopening announcement within 24 hours on April 18, 2026 after firing on a tanker amid ceasefire talks.
Severe petrochemical shortages persist in Iran following military strikes, with selective global internet access approved for businesses on April 21, 2026.
January 2026 protests in Iran resulted in at least two confirmed deaths: a 32-year-old protester in Mashhad and a 14-year-old near Tehran.
One hundred days after January 2026 protests (as of April 2026), Iran's economy has sharply deteriorated with mass unemployment amid war.
China criticized US naval blockade actions against Iran and may serve as a mediator due to its energy interests.
Pakistan has emerged as a central mediator between the US and Iran despite facing its own internal crises.
Iranian officials warned of retaliation against US actions such as vessel seizures but have exercised restraint to avoid civilian casualties.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an acute crisis as the April 22 ceasefire expiration deadline approaches with diplomatic talks stalled; the regime simultaneously confronts military brinkmanship (tanker blockade-running, vessel seizures, judiciary calls for force readiness), economic freefall ($500M daily losses), and 100+ days of sustained internal unrest, while Pakistan mediates and China signals concern over US blockade actions.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Iran's UN ambassador signaled willingness to resume talks in Islamabad conditional on US blockade lifting on April 22, 2026, as the ceasefire expiration deadline approached with negotiations stalled and military tensions escalating.
